criteria.name: Problem Clarification
criteria.description: Ability to clearly define the problem in terms of database indexing and query optimization.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Student articulates a clear, specific problem statement related to indexing and query optimization, identifying relevant aspects like query types or indexing methods.
criteria.poor_indicator: Student provides a vague or overly broad problem statement that lacks focus on indexing or query optimization specifics.

criteria.name: Objective Identification
criteria.description: Ability to identify the objectives or goals of optimizing the database query.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Student clearly identifies objectives such as reducing query execution time or optimizing index usage.
criteria.poor_indicator: Student fails to specify clear objectives, or objectives are unrelated to query optimization.

criteria.name: Assumption Articulation
criteria.description: Ability to articulate assumptions about the database environment or query constraints.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Student effectively identifies and articulates assumptions, such as data distribution or query frequency.
criteria.poor_indicator: Student overlooks key assumptions or makes incorrect assumptions about the database environment.

typical_errors: Ignoring the role of indexing in query performance.
Failing to mention specific query optimization techniques.
Misunderstanding the impact of data distribution on indexing.

criteria.name: Error Detection
criteria.description: Ability to detect errors or inefficiencies in AI-generated indexing strategies.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Student accurately identifies inefficiencies or incorrect index usage in AI-generated solutions.
criteria.poor_indicator: Student overlooks significant errors in index choice or fails to recognize inefficiencies.

criteria.name: Relevance Assessment
criteria.description: Ability to assess the relevance of AI-generated query optimization suggestions.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Student correctly evaluates the relevance of suggestions based on query patterns and database schema.
criteria.poor_indicator: Student accepts irrelevant or ineffective optimization suggestions without critical evaluation.

criteria.name: Performance Impact Analysis
criteria.description: Ability to analyze the impact of proposed indexing or optimization strategies on query performance.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Student provides a well-reasoned analysis of how the strategies will affect query performance metrics.
criteria.poor_indicator: Student fails to analyze or incorrectly assesses the performance impact of the strategies.

typical_errors: Missing indexing errors that lead to full table scans.
Misjudging the relevance of an optimization technique for a given query.
Neglecting to consider index maintenance costs in performance analysis.

criteria.name: Correction Command Precision
criteria.description: Ability to provide precise and actionable correction commands to the AI.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Student gives clear, specific commands that effectively guide the AI to improve indexing or optimization strategies.
criteria.poor_indicator: Student provides vague or non-actionable commands that do not help refine the AI's approach.

criteria.name: Iterative Improvement
criteria.description: Ability to iteratively refine AI output based on performance feedback.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Student successfully iterates on AI suggestions, showing a clear trajectory of improvement in query performance.
criteria.poor_indicator: Student fails to make meaningful improvements or does not iterate based on feedback.

criteria.name: Contextual Adaptation
criteria.description: Ability to adapt commands based on specific database contexts or constraints.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Student adapts commands considering database size, query complexity, or specific constraints.
criteria.poor_indicator: Student issues generic commands without considering the specific context or constraints.

typical_errors: Providing overly broad corrections that do not target specific issues.
Failing to adjust commands based on feedback from previous iterations.
Ignoring specific database constraints when steering AI.
